This work studies data-constrained scaling, focusing on the optimal use of computational resources when unique data is limited.
An extension to the Chinchilla scaling laws takes into account the decay in value of repeated data and is fit using a large set of controlled experiments.
Scaling laws continue to hold in the multi-epoch regime, albeit with diminishing returns.
